title: Terms of Service | Praevius
text:

By accessing or using Praevius (praevius.app), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you disagree with any part of these terms, you may not access the service.

Real-time budget tracking

title: Construction Cost Control Features | Real-Time Tracking & AI Insights | Praevius
text:
Construction Cost Control Features: Real-Time Tracking & AI Insights

From real-time tracking to AI-powered forecasting. One platform, complete visibility.







Your AI Assistant


No menus to learn. No training required. Just tell Praevius what you need in plain English—or speak it on site.




Just Ask



“Show me projects over budget”


“Generate variance report for Project Alpha”


“What’s the CPI on the hospital job?”


“Approve the steel variation”





AI Handles the Rest



 Instant dashboards & reports


 Voice commands from the field


 Permission-aware actions


 Confirmation for critical tasks










Core Capabilities




 Real-Time Budget Tracking


See your budget vs. actual costs the moment data changes. No more waiting for month-end reports to discover problems. Dashboard views show project health at a glance with traffic-light status indicators.



Live cost performance index (CPI)


Budget consumption tracking


Variance alerts and thresholds


Multi-project portfolio view





 AI-Powered Reporting


Just ask for the report you need—the AI handles the rest. Generate professional variance reports in minutes, not hours. Plain English explanations of the “why” behind your numbers.



Automated variance explanations


Trend analysis and forecasting


Executive summary generation


Export to PDF, Word, or share live



AI Reports available on Professional tier and above




 S-Curve Forecasting


Earned Value Management made practical. See where your project is headed, not just where it’s been. Plan interventions before problems escalate.



Planned vs. actual vs. forecast curves


Schedule Performance Index (SPI)


Estimate at Completion (EAC) projections


What-if scenario modeling





 Change Order Management


Track every variation from inception to approval to payment with our dedicated change order management system. Never leave money on the table again. Change orders account for 8-14% of project costs—capture them all.



Complete variation register with status tracking


Automated entitlement flagging


Impact assessment and pricing tools


Approval workflow automation





 Construction Progress Claim Software


Generate accurate progress claims in minutes, not hours. Our progress claim automation eliminates the manual process of hunting through spreadsheets, recalculating percentages, and rechecking every line item.



Percentage complete calculations with audit trail


Retainage and retention tracking


Claim history and documentation


Multi-format export (UK JCT, AU SOPA, US AIA)



View pricing for progress claim features




 Schedule of Values


Client billing structures often differ from internal cost codes. SOV management bridges this gap with many-to-many mapping between your WBS and client billing formats.



Multi-format support (UK JCT/NEC, US AIA G702/G703, AU SOPA)


Cost code to SOV line mapping


Payment application tracking


Progress sync from budget data



Schedule of Values available on Scale tier




 Seamless Integrations


Connect to the tools you already use. Praevius syncs with accounting, storage, and project management platforms for a single source of truth.



Xero, QuickBooks, Sage


Google Drive, SharePoint, OneDrive


Procore (for GC collaboration)


Open API for custom connections





 Voice Commands


Speak your requests on the jobsite. “Show me Project Alpha” or “Approve the variation”—hands-free cost control that works in noisy environments.



Natural language processing


Works in noisy environments


Field-ready voice input


Permission-aware actions










Coming Soon




 Native Mobile App


Beyond responsive web—a true native experience. Offline capability, camera integration for documentation, push notifications for approvals.




 Predictive Analytics


AI that learns from your historical data to predict likely cost overruns before they happen. Early warning, not post-mortem.









Industry-Standard Variance Indicators


Instant project health visibility using the traffic light system every construction professional understands.



 On Budget

Variance within +/-5%. Project performing as planned. Continue current trajectory.



 Watch

Variance 5-10%. Early warning. Investigate and plan corrective action.



 Over Budget

Variance exceeds 10%. Immediate attention required. Intervention needed.









Frequently Asked Questions




What is AI-powered cost reporting for construction?


AI-powered cost reporting uses artificial intelligence to generate professional variance reports from natural language requests. In Praevius, you ask for the report you need in plain English — for example, “Generate variance report for Project Alpha” — and receive a complete report with plain English explanations of cost drivers, trend analysis, and executive summaries in minutes instead of hours. Available on Professional tier and above.




What integrations does Praevius support?


Praevius integrates with Xero, QuickBooks, and Sage for accounting; Google Drive, SharePoint, and OneDrive for cloud storage; and Procore for general contractor collaboration (Scale plan). Essential plan includes 1 accounting integration plus Google Drive. Professional and Scale plans include all integrations. An open API is available for custom connections.




How does construction variance tracking work?


Praevius monitors the difference between budgeted and actual project costs in real time. When costs deviate from the baseline, the traffic light system flags it immediately: green (within ±5%), yellow (5-10% variance), red (over 10%). The system calculates Cost Performance Index (CPI) live and AI-generated reports explain root causes and suggest corrective actions.




Can I use voice commands on a construction site?


Yes. Praevius supports natural language voice input designed for noisy construction environments. Speak commands like “Show me Project Alpha status” or “Approve the variation” for hands-free cost control in the field. All voice actions are permission-aware with confirmation for critical operations.




What is Earned Value Management (EVM) in construction?


Earned Value Management is a project management methodology that integrates scope, schedule, and cost to measure project performance. Praevius implements EVM through S-curve forecasting, tracking planned vs. actual vs. forecast cost curves, calculating Schedule Performance Index (SPI) and Cost Performance Index (CPI), and projecting Estimate at Completion (EAC). What-if scenario modelling helps plan interventions before problems escalate.




How does construction change order management work?


Change orders (variations) account for 8-14% of project costs. Praevius tracks every variation from inception to approval to payment through a complete variation register with status tracking, automated entitlement flagging, impact assessment and pricing tools, and approval workflow automation. This ensures no revenue is left on the table.




What is progress claim automation?


Progress claim automation eliminates the manual process of creating payment applications. Praevius calculates percentage complete from your budget data, tracks retainage and retention, maintains a full audit trail, and exports claims in multiple contract formats including UK JCT, Australian SOPA, and US AIA G702/G703. What used to take hours of spreadsheet work takes minutes.









Ready to Take Control?


Start your free trial and experience all features. Request a demo or learn about our team.

 Start Free Trial

title: Construction Cost Variance Tracking | Real-Time Monitoring Guide | Praevius
text:
Construction Cost Variance Tracking: A Real-Time Monitoring Guide

Detect cost overruns before they escalate. Practical guide to variance tracking for construction subcontractors and QS professionals.







Construction cost variance tracking is the process of monitoring the difference between budgeted and actual project costs in real time, enabling project teams to identify overruns before they escalate. For subcontractors managing multiple projects on tight margins, effective variance tracking is the difference between profitable operations and cash flow crises.







What is Cost Variance in Construction?



Cost variance (CV) measures how project spending compares to the baseline budget at any point during construction. The formula is straightforward:


Cost Variance = Earned Value (EV) - Actual Cost (AC)


A positive CV means the project is under budget. A negative CV means over budget. But a single number lacks context — what matters is the percentage variance and whether it is trending better or worse.


The Cost Performance Index (CPI) provides a normalised view: CPI = EV / AC. A CPI of 1.0 is on budget, below 1.0 is over budget, above 1.0 is under budget. Research consistently shows that CPI rarely improves significantly after a project reaches 20% completion — making early detection critical.




 On Budget

Variance within ±5%. Project performing as planned. Continue current trajectory with routine monitoring.



 Watch

Variance 5-10%. Early warning. Investigate root causes and plan corrective action before the variance compounds.



 Over Budget

Variance exceeds 10%. Immediate attention required. Intervention needed to prevent further cost escalation.









Why Real-Time Tracking Matters




 The Month-End Shock


Most subcontractors discover cost overruns in monthly reports — weeks after the spending occurred. By then, the window for corrective action has closed. A $10,000 overrun caught in week one is manageable; the same overrun discovered at month-end has already compounded across downstream tasks.




 Change Order Revenue Leakage


Change orders represent 8-14% of total project value. Without real-time tracking, variations get missed, under-valued, or claimed late. Every day a variation goes untracked is potential revenue lost — and most subcontractors lack the systems to flag entitlements automatically.




 Cash Flow Forecasting Blind Spots


Only 12% of contractors get paid on time. Accurate variance tracking feeds directly into cash flow forecasting — you cannot predict next month’s cash position if you do not know this month’s true cost position. Late discovery of overruns cascades into incorrect progress claims and delayed payments.




 Compounding Errors


Construction cost variances compound. A 3% overrun in foundations affects every subsequent trade. Without continuous monitoring, small variances accumulate into major overruns that consume the project’s contingency before anyone notices.









How to Track Cost Variances Effectively




 Establish a Baseline Budget


Every variance is measured against a baseline. Lock your project budget at tender acceptance, including all trade packages, preliminaries, and contingency. Without a clear baseline, variance tracking becomes meaningless — you are comparing actuals to a moving target.




 Connect Your Accounting Data


Manual data entry introduces errors and delays. Connect your accounting platform (Xero, QuickBooks, or Sage) so that actual costs flow into your tracking system automatically. Praevius syncs with major accounting platforms to eliminate the gap between spending and reporting.




 Define Variance Thresholds


Set thresholds that trigger alerts — the traffic light system (±5% green, 5-10% yellow, >10% red) is an industry standard. Different project types may warrant different thresholds: a high-margin fitout can absorb more variance than a competitive tender won at 2% margin.




 Automate Variance Alerts


Do not rely on someone remembering to check the numbers. Automated alerts ensure that threshold breaches are flagged the moment they occur — not at the next project meeting. Real-time CPI monitoring catches trends before they become crises.




 Generate Variance Reports with Context


Numbers without context do not drive decisions. Effective variance reports explain the why behind the numbers — which cost codes are driving the overrun, whether the variance is a one-off or a trend, and what corrective actions are available. Praevius uses AI to generate these explanations automatically in plain English, turning raw data into actionable intelligence.









Common Cost Variance Indicators




 Cost Performance Index (CPI)


CPI = Earned Value / Actual Cost


The most reliable indicator of final project cost. A CPI of 0.90 means you are getting 90 cents of planned work for every dollar spent — a 10% overrun. CPI below 1.0 at 20% project completion rarely recovers without significant intervention.




 Schedule Performance Index (SPI)


SPI = Earned Value / Planned Value


Measures schedule efficiency. SPI below 1.0 means behind schedule. Schedule delays often cause cost overruns through extended prelims, acceleration costs, and liquidated damages. Tracking SPI alongside CPI gives the full picture.




 Estimate at Completion (EAC)


EAC = Budget at Completion / CPI


Projects the final cost based on current performance. If your $1M project has a CPI of 0.85, EAC projects a final cost of $1.18M — an $180K overrun. This is the number that drives management decisions and client conversations.




 Variance at Completion (VAC)


VAC = Budget at Completion - EAC


The expected budget overrun or underrun at project completion. A negative VAC means the project will exceed its budget if current trends continue. This metric is essential for contingency management and early stakeholder communication.









Frequently Asked Questions




What is an acceptable cost variance in construction?


A cost variance within ±5% of the baseline budget is generally considered acceptable and indicates the project is performing as planned. Variances between 5-10% are early warnings that require investigation. Variances exceeding 10% require immediate corrective action. These thresholds can be adjusted based on project size, complexity, and contractual requirements.




How often should you check cost variance on a construction project?


Cost variance should be monitored continuously with real-time data where possible. At minimum, review cost variance weekly during active construction phases and at every progress claim milestone. Monthly reviews are insufficient for catching overruns early — by the time a month-end report reveals a problem, the opportunity to correct course has often passed.




What causes cost variance in construction projects?


Common causes include: untracked change orders and variations (accounting for 8-14% of project value), scope creep without corresponding budget adjustments, material price escalation, labour productivity differences from estimates, rework due to quality issues, weather delays, and subcontractor claims. Many of these can be detected early through real-time variance tracking before they compound.




What is Cost Performance Index (CPI) in construction?


Cost Performance Index (CPI) is the ratio of earned value to actual cost: CPI = EV / AC. A CPI of 1.0 means the project is on budget. A CPI below 1.0 means the project is over budget (for example, CPI of 0.85 means you are getting 85 cents of work for every dollar spent). CPI above 1.0 means under budget. CPI is considered the most reliable early indicator of final project cost in Earned Value Management.




How do you calculate cost variance in construction?


Cost Variance (CV) = Earned Value (EV) minus Actual Cost (AC). A positive CV means under budget; a negative CV means over budget. For percentage: CV% = (CV / EV) × 100. For example, if a task with a $100,000 budget is complete but actually cost $112,000, the CV is -$12,000 or -12%, indicating a significant overrun requiring immediate attention.









Track Variances Before They Escalate


Praevius gives you real-time cost variance tracking with AI-powered reporting, traffic light indicators, and automated alerts. Built for subcontractors and QS professionals.


 Start Free Trial See All Features


30-day free trial. No credit card required.

title: About Praevius | AI Cost Control for Construction Subcontractors
text:
About Praevius: AI Cost Control for Construction Subcontractors

20 years of construction cost management expertise, now powered by AI.







The Story



Praevius (from Latin “praevius” meaning “going before”) was born from a simple observation: construction subcontractors are drowning in spreadsheets while enterprise platforms price them out of modern cost control.


After two decades working as a quantity surveyor across the UK, Australia, and Poland—handling everything from 500K fitouts to 50M commercial developments—the pattern became undeniable. Specialty trade contractors, the backbone of every construction project, were consistently:



Losing 3-5% margins to poor variation tracking


Wasting 10+ hours monthly on manual billing cycles


Discovering cost overruns weeks after they could have acted


Being priced out of tools designed for general contractors



Praevius is the solution: professional-grade cost control without enterprise complexity. Simple enough to learn in hours, powerful enough to replace spreadsheet chaos.








Why Quantity Surveying Software Needed to Change



Traditional quantity surveying software was designed for enterprise clients with dedicated IT teams and training budgets. That left mid-market contractors with two options: expensive platforms that required weeks of training, or spreadsheets that created more problems than they solved.


The construction industry loses billions annually to:



Spreadsheet errors - 88% of spreadsheets contain errors (KPMG study)


Poor cash flow visibility - Only 12% of contractors get paid on time


Missed variations - Change orders represent 8-14% of project value


Reactive cost control - Problems discovered weeks after they occurred



Praevius bridges this gap: professional-grade construction cost control software with mid-market pricing and learning curves measured in hours, not weeks.








Who Uses Praevius?




 Electrical Contractors


Managing 10-50 concurrent projects with complex variation tracking and progress billing requirements. Track every cable run, switchboard, and conduit installation.




 Mechanical & Plumbing Subcontractors


Tracking variations across multiple job sites with different billing cycles and retention requirements. Never lose track of a variation again.




 Growing QS Practices


Quantity surveying practices serving multiple clients simultaneously need professional tools that scale without enterprise complexity.




 Trade Contractors Outgrowing Spreadsheets


Specialty trades who’ve hit the limits of Excel but don’t need—or can’t afford—Procore-level solutions.




Whether you’re a specialty contractor in the UK, Australia, or expanding internationally, Praevius adapts to your progress claim requirements and local billing formats.







Our Philosophy




 Proactive, Not Reactive


The best cost control happens before problems arise. Praevius anticipates variances, flags anomalies, and surfaces insights—so you can act, not just react.




 Built for Construction


Not accounting software with construction labels. Every feature designed around real QS workflows: progress claims, variations, retainage, percentage complete.




 AI as Assistant


AI enhances your expertise, it doesn’t replace it. Specific, measurable outcomes—not vague promises. You make the decisions; Praevius handles the grunt work.









Construction Expertise Built In




 20 Years QS Experience


Founded by practicing quantity surveyors who understand real-world construction cost management challenges—not software developers guessing what you need.




 International Projects


Experience across UK, Australia, and Poland informs support for multiple billing formats and contract types (JCT, NEC, AIA, SOPA).




 AI-First Approach


Leveraging Claude AI for intelligent reporting while keeping construction professionals in control. AI enhances your expertise—it doesn’t replace it.









Part of BIM Takeoff




 BIM Takeoff


Professional BIM 5D cost estimation and quantity surveying services. 20 years of international experience across UK, Australia, and Poland. Delivering quantity takeoffs with +/-5% accuracy in 3-10 days.

Visit BIM Takeoff



 Praevius.app


The cost control intelligence layer. Where BIM Takeoff delivers project-specific expertise, Praevius provides the ongoing visibility and control that keeps projects profitable.

Start Free Trial








Ready to Work Smarter?


Start your free trial today. Experience construction cost control done right. Explore our features or view pricing options.

 Start Free Trial

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
Overview
Praevius (“we”, “our”, “us”) is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your information when you use praevius.app.

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
Information We Collect

Information You Provide

Account Information: Name, email address, company name, job title
Billing Information: Payment details (processed securely via Stripe)
Project Data: Cost data, budgets, variations, and other construction information you enter
Communications: Messages, support requests, and feedback



Information Collected Automatically

Usage Data: Features used, pages visited, time spent
Device Information: Browser type, operating system, device type
Log Data: IP address, access times, referral URLs

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
How We Use Your Information
We use your information to:

Provide and improve our services
Process payments and manage subscriptions
Send service-related communications
Provide customer support
Analyse usage patterns to improve the platform
Comply with legal obligations

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
Data Security
We implement industry-standard security measures including:

Encryption in transit (TLS 1.3)
Encryption at rest (AES-256)
Regular security audits
Access controls and authentication
Secure data centres (EU/UK based)

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
Data Retention
We retain your data for as long as your account is active. Upon account deletion:

Account data: Deleted within 30 days
Backup data: Deleted within 90 days
Anonymised analytics: May be retained indefinitely

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
Your Rights
Under GDPR and UK Data Protection Act 2018, you have the right to:

Access your personal data
Rectify inaccurate data
Erase your data (“right to be forgotten”)
Restrict processing
Data portability
Object to processing

To exercise these rights, contact us at info@praevius.app.

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
Third-Party Services
We use the following third-party services:

Stripe: Payment processing
AWS/GCP: Cloud infrastructure
Analytics: Usage analytics (anonymised)

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
Cookies
We use essential cookies for authentication and session management. Optional analytics cookies require your consent.

title: Privacy Policy | Praevius
text:
Changes to This Policy
We may update this policy periodically. Significant changes will be communicated via email or in-app notification.
